info.name: Stock Market
info.description:
Stock Market Platform API collection for the Eurisko Backend Academy final project.

This collection contains the full REST API test suite for the Stock Market Platform backend. Requests are organized by service and domain, including member authentication, admin/CMS authentication, admin users, member management, stocks, wallet operations, orders, portfolio, price alerts, analytics, FastAPI analytics service debugging, system alerts, audit logs, and Stripe webhook testing.

The main NestJS API uses:  
{{base_url}} = [http://localhost:3000/api](http://localhost:3000/api)

The standalone FastAPI analytics service uses:  
{{analytics_url}} = [http://localhost:8000](http://localhost:8000)

The Socket.IO realtime server uses:  
{{socketUrl}} = [http://localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000)

Before testing:

1. Start Docker services with docker compose up -d.
    
2. Initialize the MongoDB replica set if needed.
    
3. Copy Documentation/env/.env.example to .env and fill local values.
    
4. Run npm run seed.
    
5. Start the NestJS app with npm run start:dev.
    
6. Start Stripe webhook forwarding if testing deposits:  
    stripe listen --forward-to localhost:3000/api/payments/stripe/webhook
    

Authentication:

- Member routes use {{member_token}}.
    
- Admin routes use {{admin_token}}.
    
- Analyst routes use {{analyst_token}}.
    
- Support routes use {{support_token}}.
    

Some login requests include scripts that save tokens automatically into the active Postman environment.

Important:

- Do not commit real tokens, passwords, Stripe secrets, SMTP credentials, or production values.
    
- Example values are for local development and testing only.
    
- FastAPI internal analytics requests are included for debugging and service verification. Protected analytics testing should be done through the NestJS /analytics routes.
    
- Stripe webhook success testing requires Stripe CLI forwarding and a valid STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET in the local .env file.
